title: Active Shooter Response (Code Silver)

contact_extensions.national_center: National Center for Emergency and Health Crisis Management

plan_procedures.action: When the security operations room receives a report of an armed person inside the healthcare facility, it must rapidly verify the source of the report and the exact location of the armed person.
Collect all available security information regarding the armed person, including gender, age, appearance, clothing description, type of weapon, and the number of injured or hostages if present.
Immediately notify the relevant specialized authorities through the approved emergency channels.

Announce Code Silver through the internal overhead speaker system and repeat the announcement several times, while also sending a signal through wireless communication devices to the security teams assigned for emergency response.
Notify facility leadership immediately and provide continuous updates on incident developments.
Prevent patients, staff, and visitors from approaching or entering the location where the armed person is present.
Communicate with the armed person cautiously and carefully when required, without direct confrontation unless directed by the competent authorities.
Attempt to reach injured persons together with medical staff for safe transfer while maintaining the highest standards of protection and safety.
Secure exit routes for persons present in the incident zone and guide evacuation or protected movement according to the evolving situation.
Coordinate and cooperate with specialized security teams and relevant authorities until the incident is fully concluded.
Prepare the required reports, document the incident, and submit the information according to the approved procedures.
Provide the National Center for Emergency and Health Crisis Management with all required information related to the incident through the responsible authority in the facility.

learning_objectives: Verify and escalate an armed-person incident using the correct Code Silver procedure.
Collect critical suspect and scene information and relay it to specialized authorities and facility leadership.
Control access to the danger area and protect patients, staff, and visitors from unnecessary exposure.
Support safe access to injured persons and maintain protected movement routes when conditions allow.
Coordinate with specialized authorities and complete reporting and external notification requirements after the incident.
